steps.title: Find the y-intercept by setting x = 0
steps.content:
The y-intercept occurs where $x = 0$. Substitute into the equation:
$$y = 500(3)^{2(0)} = 500(3)^0 = 500 \cdot 1 = 500$$
The y-intercept is at the point $(0, 500)$.

steps.title: Interpret what x = 0 means and match the answer
steps.content:
The problem states that $x$ represents the number of six-month periods starting from the end of January 1991.

When $x = 0$, zero periods have elapsed, meaning we're at the starting point: the end of January 1991.

At this initial moment, there were an estimated $y = 500$ subscribers.

This matches choice B — B is correct.
